source.multipart_basis: The original sample already specifies body_count=assembly with 'each link and joint as separate sub-bodies'. Multipart decomposition formalizes the links, joint actuators, distal oscillating blade tool, base mount, and servo-drive fasteners as distinct part families.

full_prompt:
Design the ASABE/USDA CN14 Farm Robotics 4-axis crop-row weeding arm end-effector as a multi-part assembly, explicitly modeling the stainless-steel articulated arm as the physical collection of links, joint actuators, the oscillating blade tool, the platform base mount, and the servo-drive fastener sets. The original prompt already calls out 'deliver STEP AP242 assembly with each link and joint as separate sub-bodies'; this multipart sidecar formalizes that decomposition and adds the blade tool and base-mount families.

The assembly consists of six part families: (A) three Type 304 stainless-steel links with lengths 250 mm + 250 mm + 150 mm forming the articulated arm; (B) four NEMA-17 frameless servo actuators (2.0 N-m continuous torque each) located at the base, shoulder, elbow, and wrist joints; (C) the distal oscillating blade tool, 0.6 kg tool steel blade with 40 mm vertical amplitude at up to 5 Hz drive; (D) the base mount that attaches the arm to the field-robot platform chassis; (E) servo-drive mounting fasteners and joint hardware (shoulder bolts, bearings, M5 grade-8.8 fasteners) that connect the servos to the links; (F) the ball-bearing / cross-roller assemblies that accommodate rotation at each joint.

Geometry inherits the original sample verbatim: link lengths 250 mm + 250 mm + 150 mm, 4 axes, blade vertical amplitude 40 mm at up to 5 Hz, row spacing 0.76 m, in-row 0.30 m, crop stem 8 mm.

Material inherits verbatim: Type 304 stainless-steel links (yield 205 MPa, E 193 GPa, density 8000 kg/m^3); tool-steel blade at 0.6 kg.

Loads inherit verbatim: LC1 worst inertia + soil reaction at the distal blade tip (80 N soil reaction at 40 m/s^2 blade acceleration at 5 Hz peak); LC2 accidental strike 50 N axial along the longest link; LC3 modal separation with target minimum 15 Hz (3x the 5 Hz forcing).

Pass/fail criteria inherit verbatim from the original and apply at the assembly level: (R1) max von Mises in stainless-steel links under LC1 <= 125 MPa (Type 304 yield 205 / SF 1.65); (R2) end-effector tip positioning error at 5 Hz <= 5 mm; (R3) total end-effector mass <= 3.0 kg; (R4) first-mode buckling load factor of longest link under LC2 50 N axial strike >= 3.0; (R5) first natural frequency of assembled arm >= 15 Hz. Added assembly-level targets under fem_check_policy.assembled_only: joint DCR at each servo-to-link interface and bearing DCR at each joint must be reported; per-part stresses are reported for diagnostic purposes but do not gate pass/fail.

Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Vibration (modal) analysis is required for R5. Buckling analysis is required for R4. A multibody-dynamics solver (MSC Adams, Simscape Multibody, PyBullet) is required outside the FEA to derive joint-level inertia + reaction forces feeding each link's FEA. Deliver STEP AP242 of the assembly with each body (each link, each servo, blade tool, base mount, each bearing, each fastener) as a distinct solid tagged with part_family (A..F) and instance_index, plus a motion and structural report listing max von Mises, tip positioning error, total mass, first buckling load factor, first natural frequency, assembly mass, and joint / bearing DCR.
short_prompt:
Design the ASABE/USDA CN14 Farm Robotics 4-axis crop-row weeding arm as a multi-part assembly (three stainless-steel links, four NEMA-17 servos, oscillating blade tool, base mount, fasteners, joint bearings).

Link lengths 250 + 250 + 150 mm; 4 axes; row spacing 0.76 m, in-row 0.30 m, crop stem 8 mm; blade 40 mm vertical amplitude at up to 5 Hz drive. Type 304 stainless steel yield 205 MPa, E 193 GPa, density 8000 kg/m3; tool-steel blade 0.6 kg.

Load cases: LC1 worst inertia + soil reaction 80 N at blade tip with 40 m/s2 at 5 Hz peak; LC2 accidental strike 50 N axial on longest link; LC3 modal target min 15 Hz.

Pass/fail: max von Mises in links <= 125 MPa (yield 205 / SF 1.65); tip positioning error <= 5 mm; total end-effector mass <= 3.0 kg; first buckling factor >= 3.0; first natural frequency >= 15 Hz; FEM at assembled level; joint + bearing DCR <= 1.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Joint-level inertia and reaction forces are solved via rigid multibody dynamics, then fed to FEA per link.

prompt.title: 4-axis agricultural weeding arm end-effector (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part 4-axis stainless-steel weeding arm: 3 links (A) + 4 NEMA-17 servos (B) + oscillating blade tool (C) + base mount (D) + fasteners (E) + bearings (F). Link lengths 250+250+150 mm, blade 0.6 kg at 5 Hz. Verified at assembly level: max von Mises <= 125 MPa, tip positioning <= 5 mm, mass <= 3.0 kg, buckling factor >= 3.0, first freq >= 15 Hz, joint/bearing DCR <= 1.0.

parts.name: stainless_link
parts.role: Type 304 stainless-steel link (one of three) forming the 250 + 250 + 150 mm articulated arm; carries joint-to-joint bending + torsion flow under LC1 inertia + soil reaction

parts.name: nema17_servo
parts.role: NEMA-17 frameless servo at each of the 4 joints (base, shoulder, elbow, wrist) providing continuous 2.0 N-m drive torque

parts.name: oscillating_blade_tool
parts.role: distal tool-steel blade tool, 0.6 kg mass, oscillates vertically 40 mm at up to 5 Hz; engages the soil with 80 N reaction under LC1

parts.name: base_mount
parts.role: base-mount bracket fastened to the mobile platform chassis; transfers all arm reactions into the platform

parts.name: joint_fastener
parts.role: shoulder bolts + M5 grade-8.8 fasteners that tie each servo to its adjacent link and secure the base mount to the platform

parts.name: joint_bearing
parts.role: ball-bearing or cross-roller bearing assembly at each of the 4 joints; accommodates rotation and transfers radial/thrust loads from the servo into the link
parts.material_hint: commercial deep-groove ball bearing or cross-roller

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: approximately 650 x 200 x 200 mm in folded configuration
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: arm coordinates: +x along base axis, +y row-direction, +z up

material.links: Type 304 stainless steel, yield 205 MPa, E 193 GPa, density 8000 kg/m3

load_cases.name: worst_inertia_plus_soil_reaction
load_cases.application: distal blade tip at 5 Hz peak

objective.primary: pass stress, positioning, and resonance limits under assembled_only FEM check

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: joint bolt DCR per grade-8.8 bolt capacity + bearing dynamic/static load capacity per manufacturer
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam for links, solid for joints

interfaces.load_path: servo motor shaft coupled to link via shoulder bolt + key; transfers servo torque into link rotation
bearing races press-fit into link bore; accommodates joint rotation and transfers radial/thrust loads
distal link wrist coupled to oscillating blade tool via bolted interface with oscillating drive take-off
base mount bolted to the platform chassis via M5 grade-8.8 fasteners; transfers all arm reactions
servo body bolted to link flange via M5 grade-8.8 fasteners

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Articulated robotic arms are inherently multi-link multi-servo assemblies; the multipart sidecar formalizes the links / servos / blade / base / fasteners / bearings decomposition that the original sample already calls out with body_count=assembly.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact fastener count and bearing type are team-selected within the 3 kg mass budget and servo torque capacity.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
